meaning: n.海市蜃楼,雾气,幻想,妄想
examples: It hovered before his eyes like the mirage of an oasis.
Our honeymoon was two days in Las Vegas at a luxurious hotel called Le Mirage.
Because light traveling down from a summit passes through many such layers, a surveyor could sight a mirage rather than the peak itself.
The players: entertainment and energy mogul Marvin Davis, the Pritzker family and MGM-Mirage owner Kirk Kerkorian.
They are now trying to sell everything to MGM-Mirage before being stripped of their license.
